model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With only 256 fixed steps (<1 epoch), the current setup is likely under-training. Increasing Adam learning rate from 5e-4 to 1e-3 is a minimal, high-leverage early-convergence change that often improves validation loss in short-budget MLP training without adding complexity.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 1","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current MLP is relatively large for a strict 256-step budget and may be under-optimized. Reducing depth from 2 to 1 should improve optimization speed and stability in this short-horizon regime, potentially lowering validation loss despite lower capacity.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 2","old":"DEPTH = 1","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The prior depth reduction clearly hurt loss under this seed/budget. Restoring two hidden layers recovers representational capacity and should undo the regression before testing other factors.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The higher learning rate consistently regressed under this workload. Reverting to 5e-4 should recover the stronger optimization regime and return to the current best-known loss region before trying other axes.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Switching from Adam to AdamW is a low-complexity optimizer-family change that often improves generalization at the same step budget by decoupling weight decay, while keeping all other settings fixed for a clean comparison.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Under a very short fixed-step budget, heavy regularization can slow useful fitting. A modest weight-decay reduction with AdamW may improve early optimization while keeping regularization in place.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Given the tiny regression from reducing weight decay, the short 256-step regime may still prefer even less regularization. Testing zero weight decay is a simple boundary check that can improve early fit in fixed-step training.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The step-budget-aligned cosine schedule caused a clear regression, so disabling scheduling should restore the stronger constant-LR behavior for this workload.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The best observed result came with AdamW at nonzero decay; zero decay was slightly worse. Restoring 1e-4 should recover the current best-performing regularization setting.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed update budget of 256 steps, increasing batch size can improve gradient quality and cover more training examples within the same number of optimizer steps, which may reduce validation loss in this short-horizon setting.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The large improvement from batch 128 suggests this fixed-step setup benefits from processing more examples per update. Testing batch 256 is the next simple scaling step to potentially improve loss further.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Performance improved strongly when increasing batch size to 256 under a fixed 256-step budget, indicating benefit from more examples per optimizer update. Testing 512 is the next minimal extension of that successful trend.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Loss has improved consistently as batch size increased under a fixed-step budget, so testing 1024 is the next direct extension of the strongest observed trend.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strongest and most consistent gain has come from increasing batch size under fixed-step evaluation. Moving to 2048 continues that proven direction with a single low-complexity change.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After scaling to very large batches, the previous learning rate may now be conservative. Increasing to 1e-3 is a simple large-batch retuning step that can accelerate convergence within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Large-batch training still improved after moving from 5e-4 to 1e-3, so a moderate further increase to 1.5e-3 is a targeted follow-up to test whether convergence can be accelerated more within the fixed step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The move to 1.5e-3 still improved loss, so pushing to 2e-3 is a reasonable final-stage learning-rate probe for this large-batch, fixed-step regime.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The increase to 2e-3 regressed validation loss, so reverting to 1.5e-3 restores the current best-performing learning-rate setting for this large-batch configuration.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 4096","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The dominant successful pattern has been steadily increasing batch size under fixed-step evaluation, with repeated gains at 128, 256, 512, 1024, and 2048. With one step remaining, testing 4096 is the highest-upside continuation of that proven trend.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}
